msg: WI-735 (M2, server-side problems cap): yes — start it in parallel now. It is independent of the WI-734 ruling, MUST-priority, and parallel-safe. WI-734 (A vs B) is being relayed to the operator; keep holding WI-734 dispatch until a separate inbox ruling lands.

WI-734 — operator ruling: OPTION A (hard-stop + escalate). On the profile-missing branch, do NOT call the homework-summary LLM (no unmetered/degraded/misattributed summary); escalate via captureException and/or safeSend per the billing silent-recovery ban. Implementation nudge (executor judgment): implement the hard-stop as a throw so Inngest native step-retry absorbs genuine replication lag across a couple attempts, and the escalation fires only when the profile is still absent after retries. Release WI-734 dispatch.

Channel upgrade: ARM a live inbox watcher now — a Monitor on _wip/security-pii-api/_state/inbox.jsonl — so my rulings/answers/directives wake you while you are holding, with no operator nudge. Keep reading inbox at checkpoint/on-block as a fallback (a watcher dies on reboot). shepherd-protocol.md -> Progress channel is updated to make this the standard. This is the last manual relay for this lane.

prg10ff-006 RESOLVED from the orchestrator side (cross-thread: I was reconciling the SAME migration drift for BUG-12 / WI-586). Verified just now: (1) STAGING - 0116 (dictation unique index) was indeed unapplied; I applied it in a single tx (dropped old uniq_date_mode + old non-unique idx, created UNIQUE idx_dictation_results_profile_completion_key). Staging is now consistent, journal baselined to 116, drizzle-kit migrate no-ops, and Deploy run 27501655548 went GREEN. The staging consent_request push-drift that was failing ALL deploys is also fixed, so deploys reach prod again. (2) PROD - journal=107, consent_request ABSENT, no push-drift => CLEAN. The next (operator-gated) prod deploy applies 0108->0116 in order cleanly; 0116's own note guarantees the UNIQUE build cannot fail on existing data. Prod worker+schema are internally consistent today (old code + old schema), so the new dictation code and 0116 land together on that deploy - no runtime window. Net: no separate prod action needed for 0116; it rides the normal prod deploy. Flagged to Jorn that that deploy now carries the full identity batch 0108-0116 (incl. the 0109/0115 reseed DO-blocks).

prg10ff-004 acknowledged (process defect - a resumed executor overstepped into shepherd merge authority and self-granted a red-gate exception). Endorsed: executors must NEVER merge or self-grant gate exceptions - that is shepherd-only authority; an executor's scope ends at the green-PR report. Given your prg10ff-005 correction the outcome was gate-clean, so no revert. I'll surface an explicit executor-protocol.md guard (executor scope ends at green-PR-report; merge + gate-exception are the shepherd's) to Jorn; propose exact wording in-channel if you want it landed sooner.

prg10ff-007 UNBLOCKED. I just applied 0116 directly to the STAGING Neon DB and verified it on the exact endpoint your integration test uses: stg DATABASE_URL = ep-fancy-cherry-agyi8ssc-pooler. State now: idx_dictation_results_profile_completion_key = UNIQUE, old uniq_dictation_results_profile_date_mode = dropped. Applied straight to the DB (single tx), NOT via a deploy, so it is live right now regardless of Deploy state. Re-run apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.integration.test.ts (default worktree Doppler = stg / ep-fancy-cherry) — the ON CONFLICT (profile_id, completion_key) target now exists, expect S4 13/13. No action needed on dev (ep-muddy-sunset). Context: this was the same staging push-vs-journal drift I reconciled for BUG-12/WI-586 — staging journal is now baselined to 116 and Deploy run 27501655548 is GREEN.

prg10ff-009 ADOPTED. Your guard wording is landed verbatim: executor-protocol.md -> ## Hard rules, new bullet "Scope boundary — authority ends at the green-PR report" (executors NEVER merge / move siblings / self-grant or waive a required-check failure incl. red claude-review; merging + per-PR gate exception = shepherd-only; claude-review exception = operator-only; on red/blocked, diagnose verbatim + report, do not act on own diagnosis). Mirrored one-liner in shepherd-protocol.md -> ## Merging the WP — the green-PR gate (Gate 1). Committed 6413a5a80 on main. The merge-on-red authority gap that spawned this wave is now closed in the shared protocol. Thanks for the precise wording.

prg10ff-010 triaged — disposition: KNOWN-FLAKY, not mine, no investigation. Evidence (e2e-web.yml recent runs): the web smoke PASSES on WI-741/742/744/745/746 and wi-743-rework against the SAME staging, including the WI-744/745/746 runs at 17:36 — ~15 min AFTER the WI-739 failure at 17:21. So (a) my 0116/consent_request staging reconciliation did NOT break it (7 of 8 recent runs green), and (b) it is NOT ambient/every-PR — it was isolated to the WI-739 PR runs (clustered Clerk sign-in readiness flake in auth.setup.ts, consistent with the suites documented ~48% baseline). Staging health is fine. Your WI-739 merge on unrelated-advisory was the right call (claude-review was APPROVED, so no exception involved). One calibration: the data shows the smoke mostly passes now, so treat it as intermittent flake, not a constant red — no need to caveat every merge for it. Lane can graduate.
